result:
If I could change one thing, I'd make curiosity a biological imperative for humans the way hunger or thirst is now. Not the polite, surface-level kind people put on for social media or TED Talks, but the relentless, uncomfortable version that makes you question your own beliefs, your tribe's stories, and the comfortable lies you tell yourself.

Right now, most people default to whatever narrative reduces anxiety or gives them status. That single switch would cascade into everything else—better science, less tribal warfare, slower adoption of obviously stupid ideas, and maybe even a species that doesn't keep repeating the same civilizational mistakes. Everything else worth fixing probably flows from that.

message.reasoning_content: If I could change the world in one way, I'd make every person have an unquenchable drive to understand reality as it is, not as they wish it to be. That way, decisions get better, lies crumble, and we edge closer to figuring out the universe.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
